title: OptimizationRecord
description: A formal record of a single optimization hypothesis, experiment, and outcome within the Tribunus Inference Research Evidence Plane. Every optimization begins with a written hypothesis and ends with a classification.

optimization_id.description: Unique optimization identifier in the format OPT-NNNN.

title.description: Short human-readable description of the optimization (e.g. 'FlashAttention-3 for decode kernel').

subsystem.description: The inference subsystem this optimization targets.

target_pipeline.description: Stage IDs within the inference pipeline that this optimization directly affects. These reference pipeline-stage identifiers defined elsewhere in the research plane.

current_implementation.description: Description of the production implementation currently in use that this optimization seeks to replace or augment.

proposed_replacement.description: Description of the replacement technique, kernel, algorithm, or architecture being proposed.

bottleneck_hypothesis.description: Statement of the bottleneck this optimization addresses, including the reasoning chain linking the observed or predicted bottleneck to the proposed change.

expected_mechanism.description: Explanation of how the proposed replacement achieves its effect — the causal mechanism, not just the empirical expectation.

expected_primary_effect.description: Quantitative expectation of the primary improvement metric (e.g. 'reduce decode token latency by >= 15% at batch size 8 on M2 Ultra').

possible_regressions.description: Known or suspected ways the optimization could degrade performance, increase memory, harm numerical accuracy, or introduce latency tails.

source_commits.description: Commit SHAs in the Tribunus repository that implement the optimization. At least 7-character prefixes are accepted.

feature_flags.description: Feature flag identifiers or environment variable names that gate this optimization at runtime.

fallback_path.description: Description of the path the runtime takes when the optimization is disabled, deactivated, or rolled back. Documents what reverting the feature flag restores.

deployability_class.description: Deployment surface and risk tier that this optimization belongs to. Determines the review, testing, and rollout requirements.

correctness_gates.description: Names of correctness gates that must pass before this optimization can be promoted. Gate names reference specific validation procedures defined in the research plane.

safety_gates.description: Names of safety gates that must pass before this optimization can be promoted. Safety gates cover numerical accuracy, memory safety, error handling, and rollback readiness.

experiment_ids.description: Experiment identifiers in the format EXP-NNNN that were conducted to validate this optimization. Negative results remain associated with the record; experiments are never deleted.

result_classification.description: Final classification of the optimization's experimental outcome after all evidence has been collected and reviewed.

final_decision.description: Human-readable summary of the final decision: what was decided, why, and any follow-up actions or conditions attached.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this record was first created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent update to this record. Must equal created_at on initial creation.
